title: Memory Segmentation
summary: Explains the memory segmentation mechanism: segment descriptors, virtual address translation, access rights, Intel descriptor tables (GDT, LDT, IDT), and privilege levels.
content_points: Virtual address = selector (13-bit table index + table bit + 2-bit RPL) + offset within segment.
Descriptor: start address, size, access rights. OS updates start address when loading segment.
Physical address = segment_base_address + offset (if access and size checks pass).
Intel: three descriptor tables – GDT (global), LDT (per-process), IDT (interrupts).
Privilege levels: CPL (process), RPL (selector), DPL (segment); access allowed iff CPL ≤ DPL and RPL ≤ DPL.
learning_objectives: Describe segment descriptors and descriptor tables
Apply the Intel privilege level rule
